subject: Continuous Random Variables
question: Suppose that the time X (in hours) required to repair a car has an exponential distribution with parameter $\lambda = 0.25$. Find (a) the cumulative distribution function of X. (b) $\Pr(X > 4)$. (c) $\Pr(X > 10 \mid X > 8)$.
explanation: (a) It is easy to see that the cumulative distribution function is $F(x) = \begin{cases} 1-e^{-x/4} & x\ge 0 \\ 0 & \text{elsewhere} \end{cases}$ (b) $\Pr(X > 4) = 1-\Pr(X\le 4) = 1-F(4) = 1-(1-e^{-4/4}) = e^{-1} \approx 0.368$. (c) By the memoryless property, we find $\Pr(X > 10 \mid X > 8) = \Pr(X > 8+2 \mid X > 8) = \Pr(X > 2) = 1-\Pr(X\le 2) = 1-F(2) = 1-(1-e^{-1/2}) = e^{-1/2} \approx 0.6065$
